Gloves of Bladecalling  
 

Lore Item No Trade
Slot: HANDS
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 2
STA: +5 INT: +5 MANA: +50
Effect: Sword of Runes (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 14.0) at Level 40
WT: 0.0 Size: TINY
Class: MAG
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Gloves of Bladecalling
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: HANDS
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 2
STA: +5 INT: +5 MANA: +50
Effect: Sword of Runes (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 14.0) at Level 40
WT: 0.0 Size: TINY
Class: MAG
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Velious Chain 2
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):0, 0, 0
Stackable:No
Lucy Entry By:Agamenoar
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-11 05:31:01
Page Updated:Sat Sep 7th, 2019

*This item is the result of a raid event.
Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 1
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Velketor's Labyrinth
NPC Name
Velketor the Sorcerer


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Velketor's Labyrinth


Uploaded November 7th, 2024 by iventheassassin
Send a correction
Post Comment
Something Wrong Here
QuoteReply
# May 26 2006 at 1:27 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1612138626376
Scholar
109 posts
OK, something is not right with this. The effect is listed 'Sword of Runes' and the link corresponds to the spell that summons old school pet toys.

I'm under impression that these gloves provide a mana free DD spell. Which is it ?

If it's mana free DD then these are still worthwhile to go get (ANY free DD amount is greater than none, is why I say still worthwhile).
Should the effect be changed to
'Shock of Swords' ? (580pt. DD spell lvl 41)


Wilee

P.S. How in the world did you numbnuts change a post about some gloves into a rant/expose' about boots that invis and Summon Companion ? LOL

Edited, Fri May 26 13:28:54 2006
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 24 2002 at 3:12 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) News for the idiots - there are gloves that cast Summon Companion.
ROFL
QuoteReply
# Oct 27 2001 at 3:20 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591769986857
12 posts
QUOTE "Yes, you do need to play a mage to know. You ever lost a pet? You ever played a pet class? If you did, you would know that if you lost a pet in a dungeon, you CANNOT use your reclaim energy items because the pet is out of range."


ROFL

2 words SUMMON COMPANION!

▲ Page top
RE: ROFL
QuoteReply
# Jan 11 2002 at 1:09 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591760381676
20 posts
You apparently don't know what your talking about either. If your pet is lost in a dungeon chances are he's aggroed on something. Summon companion will NOT work if he is aggroed.
▲ Page top
RE: ROFL
QuoteReply
# Oct 30 2002 at 10:30 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591783493403
131 posts
pets don't agro mobs anymore...
▲ Page top
RE: ROFL
QuoteReply
# Sep 24 2002 at 8:34 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591782716990
View User Forum
10 posts
Its called pathing, just cus pets are lost dosent mean its aggroed..and I think what he ment was that any smart pet user class would know to have summon companion ready just incase


kilkandark spiritguided
"I HAVE RETURNED, FEAR THE MAGI"
51 quellious
▲ Page top
Earthen Boots and These Boots
QuoteReply
# Oct 03 2001 at 4:00 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250468848
3 posts
You can put Earthen Boots in your inventory slot, and make a hotkey of it. Simply right click it.
GUESS WHAT IVA DOES: It kills pet instantly no matter how far you are away from MOB. I still have no idea why all the mages I talk to have not done this quest. (It's a level 25-30 quest in SolA) Why would any mage wear these gloves? Look at the mana bonus. Most mages (even my human mage) has over 200int. Over 200int you only gain a small mana bonus per int point (about 1). The sta and the fact that it's weightless is only a bonus.
Are you sure the effect can only be used when worn? The earthen boots and many wands, mod rods, etc do not have to be worn to use their effects.
▲ Page top
RE: Earthen Boots and These Boots
QuoteReply
# Nov 27 2002 at 5:12 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591782777086
76 posts
these gloves arnt entirelly useless, maybe when i go exping in the hole and i dont want to scribe my spell, i might use these, oh wait, the hole, by most of the char's lvl in getting these, isnt a good place to solo. i dont use my earthen boots anymore, they died out when i replaced them with coldain skin. if i use the effect, i lose mana when i do, well lets see here, if you want to gain mana, those boots are useless cause they kill the pet, and since they fixed the pet/mob agro you have nothing to worry about, easier to just summon companion and reclaim energy, as always, STFU if you dont know squat

jeeze
QuoteReply

__DEL__1592758304735
13 posts
These gloves kick ***. The 50 mana alone on them is nice, and sword of runes is better than no effect. All i want know is where do they drop. Could this be the other mage drop by Velk (if you notice, ever singe int caster gets 2 items from Velk that are class specific, and us mages only get 1?)

hmm
QuoteReply

__DEL__1593361474987

Kinda suck in comparison to the Boots (600DD) but 50 mana gloves are pretty nice. If he dropped both that would be nice :)

▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jul 22 2001 at 9:34 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Grease that baby and shove it side ways up your candy ***.
Heh, mages should know this
QuoteReply
# Jul 22 2001 at 7:19 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756698633
2 posts
Mantria, you claim to play a mage, and if you are a mage you should know that Sword of Runes is not a DD. It summons a sword that procs a 75 damage spell against summoned creatures. These gloves do not cast a DD, they summon a magic wep, that other than the hole, or dueling another PC's or NPC's pet, are worthless. Dont get me wrong, the gloves arent too bad, +50 mana never hurt. Just thought id clarify =)

Vaaldar
56 Magician
Morell Thule
▲ Page top
RE: Heh, mages should know this
QuoteReply
# Nov 30 2003 at 12:04 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1704672973637
Guru
5,311 posts
Since Luclin came out there are more summoned mobs in the game, making the swords more useful.

Also, is there not a range of levels where our pets dual wield only if they have two weapons? It's been a while so I'm sorry I don't remember what that level range is. That's when I got the most use out of the swords.

Quick Question
QuoteReply
# Jul 20 2001 at 1:20 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592247242173
73 posts
Do you have to wearing these to use the clickable effect? I know that for Jboots you can have them in your inventory and still work.

If you don't have to wear them to use the clickable effect, can a different class trigger them? It would be handy for sk's or necro's to give swords to their pets.

I know the answer is probably, 'NO', but I like to hope...
▲ Page top
sigh...
QuoteReply
# Jul 20 2001 at 6:25 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591757241660
2 posts
It is sad that mages dont get more clickable items...

Droods get free Dot's off an item which can be had for 2-3k. we get a nice free 600 point dd which can be had for the small price of...oh wait, I forgot...gotta kill an uber mob in velks...

I think most of the tank classes out there have more clickable items than we do...

And when we do get clickable items, they summon swords, (when was the last time i summoned a sword...let alone a sword that VI nerfed.), bandages, oh and another item that summons bandages...Must be for those times when you are in battle and your tank is dying and he shouts out, "Quick whip me up some bandages!!!" cuz after the fight is over, there is no time to sit down, forget one spell, mem summon bandages, and then stand up and cast it. Oh...then sit back down, forget summon bandages, and re-mem your other spell.

Its like giving warriors a clickable item that has effect : Summon ring mail gloves. or Summon Bronze rapier...

What about right click scintilation, or instead of bandages, gift of xev?

The only useful items (besides boots of bladecalling) are the quest robes for the damage shield and the pants for burnout3. BUT that is only when soloing green dungeons and farming etc. Cuz in a serious situation, I am casting BO4 and most likely a group DS...

But fear not fellow mages. We do have at least 6 items which right click reclaim energy...

Hmmm. Maybe I should go post this on the gloves thread...

Asifos Currimcules
57 Magician
▲ Page top
Don't forget...
QuoteReply
# Jul 20 2001 at 2:44 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591757464810
156 posts
Don't forget these Uber-Boots!

Earthen Boots

Wt 2.00. +5 AC, +5 Sta, +15 HP, LORE MAGIC NODROP Effect: Invisibility versus Animals.

Why the heck they put IVA on a mage item I'll never know, but it has come in handy a few times on those rare Animal mobs that can see invis but not through IVA.
▲ Page top
RE: Don't forget...
QuoteReply
# Jul 23 2001 at 12:20 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1594570605289
24 posts
My mage is only level 14 but when I am totally out of mana having barely survived after my pet dies I would like to sit & med without some bear walking up, taking a swipe, and hearing a druid say, "It's mages like you...." Invis vs Animals mana free; I'll take it.
▲ Page top
RE: Don't forget...
QuoteReply
# Jul 20 2001 at 9:23 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249859731
1 post
Uhm unless you've played a Mage, which according to this uninformed post, you havn't. Earthen boots are a life saver for groups and mages no matter where you are.......Mage PETS go poof with ANY type of invis cast upon the pet owner. Thus these boots are perfect for any mage who wishes to ensure quick destruction of the wayward pet THUS saving their group from being trained.
BTW Give me a pair of gloves I can cast unlimited 600+dd for FREE and no mana cost I'd gladly take them. Was wondering casting time though, Sord of Runes has either a 6 second or 8 second casting time. do these gloves have that too?

Mantria
Level 53 Mage
▲ Page top
RE: Don't forget...
QuoteReply
# Jul 23 2001 at 6:40 AM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
<rolls of floor laughing>
sword of ruins? a 600 point dd? a 53 mage that doesnt even know that the 600 point dd is named Shock of Swords, not Sword of Ruins? im gonna laugh when you get these, start to solo something, then try and nuke it with the gloves..and just end up with a sword in yer inventory on your corpse
▲ Page top
RE: Don't forget...
QuoteReply
# Jul 22 2001 at 3:49 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591761912254
135 posts
Two points in response to your post...I don't need to be a mage to know that you would be dumb to use an invis to nix the pet instead of Reclaim Energy to regain at least some of the mana (which as someone else pointed out is on no less than 6 mage items, 4 of which are the focus items that most mages can get pretty early on)...second, who cares about the recast time on Sword of Runes? I doubt there will ever be more than a handful of times you will ever need to cast it in a hurry in the midst of a pitched battle. And if you did, you summon a sword, cast a dd or ds, summon a sword, cast another dd or whatever, summon a sword, etc...

To others though who are saying "why bother, that's lame, only summons a sword"...ok, so granted, not a great effect, but hell, if it saves you mana, use it! You don't have to wear the gloves ALL the time, just put them on, summon a couple of swords for your pets, or melee types on corpse runs if you are bored and sitting around the gate, whatever...would I go out of my way to buy or hunt for these gloves if I were a mage? LOL!! Not likely, though 5int and 50mana is nothing to sneeze at! (Especially given that Sable Jaune didn't bother to state where these drop or from what)
▲ Page top
RE: Don't forget...
QuoteReply
# Aug 21 2001 at 9:28 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593374958307
20 posts
Thing with the boots is if the pet is out of spell casting range I can still invis my self to poof it.
▲ Page top
RE: Don't forget...
QuoteReply
# Aug 17 2001 at 10:21 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361532627
View User Forum
54 posts
Yes, you do need to play a mage to know. You ever lost a pet? You ever played a pet class? If you did, you would know that if you lost a pet in a dungeon, you CANNOT use your reclaim energy items because the pet is out of range.
▲ Page top
RE: Don't forget...
QuoteReply
# Aug 19 2001 at 9:27 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766174364
7 posts
Yeah I gotta add that too, mages always keep some sort of invis thing around, well most mages except me, cuz I never bothered to quest for these and prefer my GEBs but I still used to have invis the spell permanently up and now have summon companion in its place... These are a lifesaver and its usually not the mage that they save, (Earthen boots I mean)
▲ Page top
RE: sigh...
QuoteReply
# Jul 20 2001 at 9:43 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757381256
26 posts
Hmm a Cliackable 600 DD, well as a Shaman I had to dish out 20K for my Bracer that only does 263, so I imagine if said item was droppable the post would be WHY should I have to pay 80K for a 600 DD item? Get a clue.. these DD Items are powerful.. As for RClick items.. Well, tanks don't have spells.. And the large part of the RClick effects from Kunark are *in Cartman voice* Weak. Especially the Crap Buffs Shamans get. But in all honesty, Tanks get no stats out of Cobalt armor etc, they get little mini spells like Shrink. Caster Gear has, say it with me.. Stuff casters can Use.. IE Mana, and Int.. Maybe some saves here and there. Just my 2 cents.

Warriors come one come all. whit this spiffy litle gadjet you shal never look for your epic again hell if it was pissible idd trow at monsters whit this in my inventory. and for those of us who dont have an epic. its nice to always have a weapon waiting for us at bank no matter how much time you die on corpse run.
